hero.statement: I turn ideas into working web services.
hero.description: I start with the problem, design an easy-to-use interface, write all the code the product needs, then help launch and grow it.

projects.title: Projects people already use
projects.description: These are not just polished screens. They are working services that can be used, updated, and improved over time.

powersketch.summary: An online editor for drawing electrical panel diagrams and preparing them for installation.
powersketch.description: PowerSketch lets an electrician build a panel much as they would at a workbench: choose real devices from the catalog, place them on the diagram, and connect them with wires. The service then creates the device list and connection table automatically, so nothing has to be copied by hand.
powersketch.audience: It is made for electricians, smart-home integrators, panel builders, commissioning engineers, and technical specialists.
powersketch.challenge: The main challenge was making a precise engineering tool feel straightforward in everyday work. When someone moves a device or wire, the pins, layers, reports, and export all need to update without losing data.
powersketch.challengeTitle: An advanced engineering tool that is easy to understand
powersketch.role: I build PowerSketch end to end: user flows, interface and data design, frontend, backend, production releases, and ongoing product support.
powersketch.approach: The diagram sits at the center of the editor. Every device has dimensions and pins, every wire has two endpoints, and every layer has an order and visibility. Change something once and the update appears in the editor, reports, and exported file.
powersketch.architecture: Diagram editing happens in the browser, so every move feels immediate. The server stores projects and the catalog and handles sign-in, shared links, and subscriptions. The user never has to think about that split—they can simply continue where they left off.
powersketch.systemTitle: One diagram, all the data you need
powersketch.constraints: Large diagrams still need to feel smooth, and an exported file needs to match what users see in the editor. Older projects must keep opening after updates, and the core tools need to work across different screen sizes.

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.title: From a new diagram to ready-to-use documents
workflow.description: Every step is connected. The user draws the diagram once, and PowerSketch reuses that information for reports, sharing, and export.

create.title: Create a project
create.description: Every diagram lives in the workspace, where it is easy to find, open, or copy.

compose.title: Build the panel
compose.description: Find the device you need in the catalog and drag it onto the diagram.

connect.title: Add the wires
connect.description: Wires connect specific device pins, keeping the diagram clear and easy to check.

report.title: Download reports
report.description: PowerSketch builds device and connection tables directly from the finished diagram.

share.title: Share the result
share.description: Send a view-only link to a client or colleague, or download an SVG for the job.

scene.title: A precise diagram
scene.description: Zoom, move, and edit the diagram without losing dimensions or connection points.

catalog.title: Devices close at hand
catalog.description: Search and clear categories make it easy to find the right model among 2,000 devices.

layers.title: A tidy workspace
layers.description: Group items into layers, hide what you do not need, and lock finished parts against accidental edits.

data.title: No duplicate work
data.description: Add devices and wires once, and they automatically appear in reports and exports.

customDevices.label: Custom devices
customDevices.title: If a device is missing, you can add it
customDevices.description: Enter its dimensions, upload an image, and place the pins. PNG, JPG, SVG, and WEBP files up to 5 MB are supported. A new model can also be suggested for the shared catalog.

reports.label: Reports
reports.title: Reports are built from the diagram
reports.description: One report lists device models, specifications, notes, and quantities. Another lists every wire with its label, cross-section, length, source, and destination. Unneeded columns can be removed before download.

handoff.label: Hand-off
handoff.title: The result is easy to review and hand off
handoff.description: Send a view-only link to a colleague or client—no account or editor access is required. For installation or printing, export the diagram as SVG with the right background, paper size, and orientation.

productModel.label: Product model
productModel.title: You can start for free
productModel.description: The editor and device catalog are included in the free plan. Plus and Professional suit people managing more projects, with higher limits, project copying, and watermark-free export.

powersketch.resultTitle: PowerSketch covers the job from start to finish
powersketch.result: Today, PowerSketch lets people create a project, build a diagram from 2,000 ready-made devices, add the wiring, and produce useful documents. A project can be shared by link or exported as SVG. Everything runs in the browser, with nothing extra to install.

planesArch.summary: A bilingual architecture studio website with project galleries, services, and educational programs.
planesArch.description: The PLANES architects website puts the studio's work first. Visitors can explore architecture and interior projects, learn about the team's services and process, join an educational program, or send an enquiry.
planesArch.audience: The website introduces the studio to future clients and gives architects and designers a way to discover Bootcamp by PLANES.
planesArch.challenge: The goal was to bring the studio's calm editorial style to the browser without taking attention away from the project photography. The team also needed a simple way to add work, translate copy, and update large galleries without a developer.
planesArch.challengeTitle: More room for architecture, without making the website hard to manage
planesArch.role: I handled the frontend architecture and responsive build, connected Strapi, localization, filters, galleries, and display themes, and prepared the website for production.
planesArch.approach: The interface is built around photography and restrained typography. There are no unnecessary decorative layers: navigation stays compact, captions do not compete with the images, and transitions make it easy to move through sections and projects.
planesArch.architecture: Nuxt renders the pages, routes, and language versions. The studio manages projects, copy, and photography in Strapi, while PostgreSQL stores the content structure. Images are delivered separately at a suitable size for each screen.
planesArch.systemTitle: The studio can update the website without touching code
planesArch.constraints: The main constraints were the weight of project galleries, the different lengths of Russian and English copy, and the need to preserve a balanced layout on phones, tablets, and wide screens.

portfolio.label: Portfolio
portfolio.title: The right project is easy to find
portfolio.description: Work is grouped into private, commercial, cultural, and competition projects. Filters narrow the list quickly, while each project pairs its gallery with clear details about the space.

content.label: Content
content.title: One CMS for projects, copy, and photography
content.description: Editors enter a project once, adding its description, details, and gallery before linking the Russian and English versions. Nuxt receives that content and turns it into the finished page.

locales.title: Russian and English
locales.description: Switch languages without losing the current section or open project.

themes.title: Three themes
themes.description: Follow the device setting or choose the light or dark theme manually.

cms.title: Easy updates
cms.description: New projects and articles are added through Strapi, without rebuilding the interface by hand.

media.title: Large galleries
media.description: Photography remains the main content and loads at a size suited to each device.

themes.label: Display
themes.title: The website adapts to the light around it
themes.description: System mode follows the device setting. Light mode keeps the feel of a printed architecture journal, while dark mode makes project photography more comfortable to browse at night.

responsive.label: Mobile
responsive.title: Photography stays just as important on a phone
responsive.description: Navigation becomes shorter, actions remain within reach, and large images adapt to the portrait screen without tiny text or horizontal scrolling.

planesArch.resultTitle: A website that can grow with the studio's portfolio
planesArch.result: PLANES architects now has a working bilingual website for projects, services, Bootcamp, and enquiries. The team can update it through the CMS, while visitors can browse the work on any device and in the theme they prefer.

capabilities.title: I can build the whole product or take on one part of it
capabilities.description: I can help define what to build, create the interface and server, connect the services you need, and get everything ready to launch.

discovery.title: Problem and first version
discovery.description: Together we work out who the product is for, what problem it solves, and what the first version needs.

interface.title: Interface
interface.description: I plan the screens and actions so that even a complex service feels easy to use.

frontend.title: Web application
frontend.description: I build the fast, accessible part of the product that people use in their browser.

backend.title: Server and integrations
backend.description: I build the server and connect payments, CRM, Telegram, and other external services.

launch.title: Launch and growth
launch.description: I put the product online, keep an eye on it, and help ship the next updates.

process.title: Projects I work on
process.description: From a focused landing page to a complex web service. I can help shape an idea, build a project from scratch, or join an existing one.

websites.title: Websites
websites.description: Landing pages, company websites, and portfolios.

products.title: New products
products.description: MVPs and custom projects, from the first version to launch.

flight.canvasLabel: Arcade game: the aircraft flies and fires automatically while the player moves left and right
flight.intro: Flight and weapons are automatic. Control the course and avoid every collision.

about.title: I think about the whole product, not just individual screens

paragraphs.product: I'm a full-stack developer, so I can build both the part people see and the server behind it. The project does not have to bounce between different specialists.
paragraphs.workflow: I enjoy understanding the real problem, building a first working version quickly, and improving it with the people who actually use it.

principles.interface: I start with the user's problem, then choose the technology.
principles.prototype: A working version is more useful than a long document.
principles.automation: I try to automate repetitive work.
principles.launch: The work is not done until the product is live.
